name: Imp Warren Entrance
description: A rocky terrain riddled with burrow-holes and narrow passages where imp colonies have tunneled for generations. The corruption here is dense and low to the ground, seeping from the warrens below.
ambient_lines: The ground is honeycombed here. You test each step before committing.
Imp chatter from below the surface — rapid, layered, impossible to parse.
A burrow entrance, perfectly circular, two hands wide. Movement just inside the darkness.
Something small throws a pebble from a distance. When you look, nothing is there.
The rock face is covered in scratch marks at consistent heights. They aren't random.
The corruption from the warrens smells acrid — sulfur cut with something sweeter.
A dead fire pit surrounded by tiny, precise arrangements of bones.
You can hear running water somewhere below. Imp warrens often route around underground streams.
Small eyes in the shadows. Too many of them. Watching with coordinated patience.
The entrance to one of the larger warrens is blocked with cork and twine. Recently sealed from outside.
An imp emerges, stares at you for three seconds, and descends back into its burrow.
Your boot displaces a rock. Below it, a careful geometric arrangement of colored stones.
The ground near the largest warren radiates warmth. The corruption source is active.
Droppings on the rock. Fresh. You've been tracked for longer than you realized.
A tattered Warden Order sash caught on a rock spur. Old blood on it.
The narrow passages between rock formations are exactly the right width for an imp. Not for you.
Chittering stops suddenly when you take three steps in a particular direction. You note which direction.
Something has arranged dozens of small skulls along the top of a ledge. They're all facing the same way.
An offering bowl filled with shiny objects — coins, buttons, a broken compass needle.
The density of imp signs increases the closer you get to the main entrance. They know you're here.

time_variants.dawn: The imps retreat at dawn's light. The warrens go quiet. It's the safest window, and it's short.
time_variants.morning: Morning keeps most imps below ground. The ones that emerge are scouts — more cautious, more organized.
time_variants.midday: Midday drives them fully underground. The warren entrance is quiet. The ground still hums.
time_variants.evening: At evening, imp activity increases. They're social at dusk. Dangerous socially.
time_variants.night: Full night is full activity. The warrens pour out. Every surface has something moving on it.

points_of_interest.name: The Main Entrance
points_of_interest.desc: The largest warren opening, three meters across and reinforced with scavenged timber. The organized construction implies generations of coordinated labor.

points_of_interest.name: The Offering Rock
points_of_interest.desc: A flat stone covered in imp offerings — objects that caught their attention. Among the trinkets, a sealed letter in Warden script.

points_of_interest.name: The Sealed Warren
points_of_interest.desc: A burrow system sealed from the outside with heavy timber and pitch. Whatever is inside, someone decided it should stay there.

arrival_scene: The ground changes before you can see the warrens — you feel the hollowness beneath your feet, the faint give of earth that has been tunneled under. Then the rock formations open up and you see the burrow-holes everywhere, at every scale, and the sense of being observed by something organized and patient settles over you like a second atmosphere.

connections.name: Warming Frost
connections.desc: Southeast, climbing the ridge. The imp density thins as the cold increases.

connections.name: Wolf-Road Edge
connections.desc: South along the rocky scree. More open ground, fewer imps.
